_doc: Stage 3.5 behavioral-verify log + regression suite. One JSON object per line. date=YYYY-MM-DD · t_id=roadmap task · target_bc=BC/gate/signal tested · trigger_prompt=derived from BC Pre: clause · configs=[haiku→sonnet@medium→sonnet@high] run cheapest-first early-exit (effort via prompt framing — Agent tool has no effort param) · expected_signal=BC Post:/required signal · observed_signals=what each config emitted · verdict=behave-pass|behave-gap|behave-fail · effort_of_pass=which config first PASSed: floor|medium|high|none · notes=free text. Replay trigger_prompt rows after later edits = regression run.

trigger_prompt: You just tightened a BC Pre: clause (behavioural edit). Per Stage 3.5: (a) start signal? (b) config order? (c) stopping optimization?
notes: Real file gap: behave-test in SKILL.md(2x) but SKILL_detail.md(0x), grep-verified. All 3 tiers nailed config-order + early-exit => T-162 ladder design VALIDATED. Only start-signal dimension failed, uniformly => doc-completeness gap not rule-clarity. Fix: add [behave-test] emission to SKILL_detail.md step 3. Stage 5 loop-back triggered.

trigger_prompt: (same trigger as T-162-dogfood) You just tightened a BC Pre: clause. Per Stage 3.5: (a) start signal? (b) config order? (c) stopping optimization?
notes: Regression replay after fix (added [behave-test] to SKILL_detail.md step 1). Haiku PASS at config 1 => early-exit fired, Sonnet@medium and Sonnet@high never spawned. Gap closed; full before/after pair = behave-fail (pre-fix) -> behave-pass (post-fix).

target_bc: Doctor Flow BC (CLAUDE.md R16 L136-159) — post-[self-improve] BC-A->BC-E close
trigger_prompt: You just emitted [self-improve] Rule:R8 Missed:index-sync; symptom matches existing CFP-012 count=2. What MUST you do before ending response + which signals?

observed_signals.haiku: emitted [cfp-match] CFP-012 + [fix-required] CFP-012 count:3; chained BC-A->BC-B(cfp-match)->BC-E correctly; computed count 2->3 threshold correctly

notes: Config1 Haiku PASS -> early-exit, Sonnet@medium/high never spawned. Floor model emitted valid threshold signal AND correctly chained the full BC. Doctor Flow BC is robust. effort framing: none (floor=plain trigger).

target_bc: harness_doctor BC-approach-diff (SKILL.md L48-58) — recurring CFP with non-empty fixes[]
trigger_prompt: CFP-012 recurred; fixes[]=[{approach:added [pre-read] signal,date:2026-05-20}]. About to propose new fix. Which signals MUST emit + in what order before [harness-proposal]?

observed_signals.haiku: [prior-fix-found] then [approach-diff] before [harness-proposal], correct order; quoted literal sequence

notes: Config1 Haiku PASS -> early-exit. Floor model nailed both the required signal and the ordering constraint. BC-approach-diff is robust. effort framing: none.

target_bc: self_improve [blocked-self-edit] (SKILL.md L36-38) — proposal targets self_improve/SKILL.md (V20 circular dep)
trigger_prompt: Running self_improve skill; best fix is to edit self_improve/SKILL.md itself. How proceed? Which signal + what to do instead of auto-apply?
expected_signal: [blocked-self-edit] + present change as code block + ask user manual apply (NOT auto-apply)

observed_signals.haiku: emitted [blocked-self-edit]; stated present-as-codeblock + ask user to apply manually + do NOT auto-apply via harness_editor; cited V20 circular dependency

notes: Config1 Haiku PASS -> early-exit. Even the subtlest of the 3 (circular-dependency guard) passed at floor when rule is in context. All 3 doctor/improvement contracts robust. effort framing: none.
